titlestmt.titleproper: Nahum (Nach) Waxman Collection of Food and Culinary Trade Cards8573
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-05-25 18:41:25 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Nahum (Nach) Waxman Collection of Food and Culinary Trade Cards
did.abstract: This collection traces the history of food, cookery, agriculture, kitchen equipment, and more through hundreds of advertising trade cards from the second half of the 19th century.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: In 1983 Nach Waxman opened the New York City book store Kitchen Arts & Letters, specializing in cookbooks, gastronomy, and food books and magazines from across the world.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: This collection traces the history of food, cookery, agriculture, kitchen equipment, and more through hundreds of advertising trade cards from the second half of the 19th century.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Physical arrangement by donor. Intellectually arranged in alphabetical order.

emph.ref: Series III. Baking: Flours and Milling
Series VI. Beverages: Fermented and Distilled
Series IX. Candy and Other Confections
Series XVII. Fruit and Fruit Products
Series XVIII. Gelatin and Gelatin Products
Series XX. Household: Kitchen Equipment and Tableware
Series XXIV. Mince Meat and Plum Pudding
Series XXVI. Preserves, Extracts, Dried Fruits, Juices, Syrups
Series XXIX. Scrapbook of Assorted Trade Cards
Series XXX. Seed and Nut Foods

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Nahum (Nach) Waxman Collection of Food and Culinary Trade Cards, #8573. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Bars, Sampling Rooms, Taverns, Alehouses, and Other Drinking Establishments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains a number of full and partial card sets for Chocolat Poulain.

did.unittitle: Milk and Butter, Cheese, Cream, Dairy Stores
Preserves, Extracts, Dried Fruits, Juices, Syrups
Great Atlantic & Pacific Tea Co. (A & P)
Great Japan and London Tea Co.
Ice Cream Freezers and Other Equipment
Other Remedies and Other Medicinal Products
Preserves, Extracts, Dried Fruits, Juices, Syrups

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For additional fruit preserves, see Box 18.

did.unittitle: Restaurants, Lunch Rooms, Dining Rooms, Coffee Rooms, Ladies' Lunch Rooms, Cafes, Coffee Saloons, Pavilions, Dining Parlors, Dining and Oyster Saloons, and Other Dining Establishments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From donor: This category unavoidably merges alimentary starches, used in cooking, and laundry starches, used in ironing. Nearly ever manufacturer produced both of these products, and much of their advertising promotes brand rather than one or the other type of starch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From donor: This category merges two pieces of equipment that were distinguished in the 19th and early 20th centuries -- stoves, primarily for heating, and ranges, used primarily for cooking.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From donor: This is a brand name, the only one given its own place in this list of categories. Thurbers', one of the leading users of handsomely designed trade cards, was the brand of H. K. & F. B. Thurber & Co., founded in 1875 and known after 1884 as Thurber, Whyland, & Co. A world-known company, with branches in London and Bordeaux, it produced and distributed a wide variety of goods, ranging from canned fruits, vegetables, and meat to soaps, coffee, dairy goods, and flavoring extracts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From donor: A special design category, cutting across a wide variety of subjects. Popular from the early days of American trade cards, these caricatures employ the images of vegetables or fruit to which are added arms, legs, and facial features to portray humanlike figures. They remain a favorite of collections.
